L1.description: The web application does not impose any restriction on the number of failed authentication attempts. An attacker can perform unlimited brute force login attempts against user accounts without any rate limiting, account lockout, or cooldown mechanism. This allows credential stuffing and brute force attacks against authentication endpoints, potentially leading to unauthorized access to any user account.

ground_truth.reason: The application lacks any mechanism to restrict excessive authentication attempts, allowing unlimited brute force attacks against user credentials without rate limiting or account lockout.
